profile.name: Dr. Md Enamul Haque
profile.title: Associate Professor in Electrical Engineering
profile.faculty: Faculty of Science Engineering and Built Environment
profile.heroSubtitle: Researcher, educator, and consultant specializing in renewable energy, electric vehicles, battery storage, and smart grid systems. 20+ years of experience across 5 countries.
profile.location: School of Engineering, Deakin University, Geelong, Victoria, Australia
profile.bio: Dr. M. E. Haque received his Ph.D. degree from the University of New South Wales, Sydney, Australia. After completing his Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering, Dr. Haque worked as an academic at the University of New South Wales (UNSW), King Saud University (Saudi Arabia), United Arab Emirates University (UAE) as Assistant Professor, and the University of Tasmania (UTAS), Hobart, Australia before joining Deakin University. Dr. Haque currently works as an Associate Professor (Electrical Engineering and Renewable Energy) in the School of Engineering, Deakin University, Australia.
profile.detailedBio: Dr. Haque's research interests include renewable energy (wind, solar PV), electric vehicles, battery and supercapacitor energy storage, digital twin for microgrid, smart grid, cybersecurity in energy systems, and power electronics applications in renewables, electric vehicle and energy storage. He has been actively involved in professional activities and is a Senior Member of IEEE. He serves as an Associate Editor of the IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion and IEEE Access. He was the founding Chair of the IEEE Industry Applications Society, Victoria and Chair of IEEE Power and Energy Society, Victoria.
fieldsOfResearch: Electrical Energy Transmission, Networks and Systems
Hybrid and Electric Vehicles and Powertrains
